_human_review.notes: SA Stage B generated/autolabeled labels were reconciled by Codex for internal benchmark cleanup; project-owner approval is still required before promotion-ready status.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Cross-border transfers: no transfer outside the Kingdom unless aligned with SDAIA guidance, with registration of the transfer and safeguards; the current test export to EU‑West is pending SDAIA reference S‑XFER‑0077 and remains disabled until approval.

Selective disclosure is prohibited: no sharing of draft earnings ranges, undisclosed port concession awards, or murabaha financing term sheets with analysts, vendors, or employees beyond a documented need‑to‑know before the Tadawul announcement.

Issuer‑relative materiality will be determined by the Disclosure Committee and recorded by Ms. Huda Al‑Sahli; cafeteria hours or facility repainting are not material, but throughput variances beyond disclosed guidance may be.
no processing of unrelated categories (e.g., biometric templates) without written instruction

Cross-border transfers: no transfer outside the Kingdom unless aligned with SDAIA guidance, with registration of the transfer and safeguards; the current test export to EU‑West is pending SDAIA reference S‑XFER‑0077 and remains disabled until approval
identification will rely on National ID/Iqama verification without storing copies beyond verification artifacts

Blackout windows apply for insiders and service providers with access, from seven days before quarter‑end until two trading days after publication of financial results on the Saudi Exchange, and during any ongoing corporate action preparation

Data subject rights: access, correction, deletion, and restriction requests
information is only “public” once announced on the Saudi Exchange (Tadawul) portal or via other CMA‑authorised broad dissemination
